records.ety: [OE. youthe, youhþe, uheðe, uweðe, eoeðe, AS. geoguð, geogoð; akin to OS. juguð, D. jeugd, OHG. jugund, G. jugend, Goth. junda. √281. See Young.]

defs: The quality or state of being young; youthfulness; juvenility.
quotes: Such as in his face Youth smiled celestial.

defs: The part of life that succeeds to childhood; the period of existence preceding maturity or age; the whole early part of life, from childhood, or, sometimes, from infancy, to manhood.
quotes: He wondered that your lordship Would suffer him to spend his youth at home.
Those who pass their youth in vice are justly condemned to spend their age in folly.

defs: A young person; especially, a young man.
quotes: Seven youths from Athens yearly sent.
It is fit to read the best authors to youth first.
